data.title: Banks' Pitch to Investors Paint X as Improving, Sources Say — WSJ
Banks Upsized X Debt Deal Following Strong Investor Demand, Sources Say — WSJ
Banks Sell $5.5 Billion of X Loans to Investors, Sources Say — WSJ
Banks Sell $5.5 Billion of X Loans After Investor Interest Surges — WSJ
data.description:
By Alexander Saeedy and Justin Baer

Elon Musk's power in Washington and the return of big advertisers like Amazon have Wall Street clamoring to get a piece of X.

Banks wrapped up the sale of $5.5 billion in debt backed by Musk's social-media company on Wednesday, people familiar with the matter said. The banks had originally planned to sell around $3 billion at around 95 cents on the dollar, The Wall Street Journal had reported. They upsized the deal after seeing demand from investors, the people said.

In the end, investors agreed to buy the loans at 97 cents on the dollar. The floating-rate debts carry an interest rate of about 11%, with borrowing costs several percentage points above even the riskiest loans on Wall Street.

The sale of X Corp.'s loans showcase the fierce animal spirits that have taken over Wall Street since Donald Trump's election in November. Investors are also keen to bet on Musk, given his proximity to President Trump and ballooning influence since the election.

Musk's social-media company stumbled when advertisers fled the platform en masse after he bought the company in 2022. While some advertisers have returned, X is still struggling to break even and had relied on infusions from Musk's corporate empire to pay its bills. But even the most stalwart credit investors believe that Musk's deep pockets and tight connections in Washington are good enough reasons to invest in his social-media company.

At a meeting on Friday with potential investors, bankers from Morgan Stanley and X's chief executive officer, Linda Yaccarino, presented a summary of X's improving financial health.

Write to Alexander Saeedy at alexander.saeedy@wsj.com and Justin Baer at justin.baer@wsj.com

data.title: MODINE MANUFACTURING CO SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Modine Manufacturing Co, a leader in thermal management technology and solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of fiscal 2024. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, showcasing significant growth in key segments and strategic initiatives aimed at enhancing profitability and market presence.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $616.8 million, increased by 10% from the third quarter of fiscal 2024, primarily due to higher sales in the Climate Solutions segment.
  • Gross profit: $149.6 million, representing a gross margin improvement of 160 basis points to 24.3%, driven by higher sales and lower cost of sales as a percentage of sales.
  • Operating income: $59.3 million, decreased by $2.4 million from the prior year, primarily due to higher SG&A and restructuring expenses.
  • Net earnings: $41.2 million, decreased from $45.1 million in the prior year, impacted by higher SG&A expenses and the absence of a gain on sale recorded in the previous year.
  • Net earnings per share – diluted: $0.76, compared to $0.83 in the prior year, reflecting the changes in net earnings.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Climate Solutions segment saw a significant increase in net sales, driven by $73.6 million in incremental sales from the acquisition of Scott Springfield Manufacturing. Data center cooling and HVAC&R products experienced substantial growth, while heat transfer products saw a decline.
  • Geographical Performance: The Americas region showed strong performance with significant sales growth, particularly in the Climate Solutions segment. Europe and Asia also contributed to the overall sales, with Europe experiencing a decrease in sales in the Performance Technologies segment.
  • Sales Units: The Climate Solutions segment reported increased sales volumes, particularly in data center cooling products, which included contributions from the Scott Springfield Manufacturing acquisition.
  • New Production Launches: The company is expanding its production capacity to support organic growth opportunities, especially within its Data Center Cooling business, indicating a strategic focus on scaling operations to meet demand.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects continued growth in the Climate Solutions segment, driven by the integration of recent acquisitions and organic sales growth in data center cooling. The strategic shift towards higher-margin technologies and the sale of automotive businesses in Germany are expected to optimize resources and improve profitability.

SEC Filing: MODINE MANUFACTURING CO [ MOD ] - 10-Q - Feb. 05, 2025

data.title: Toronto Transit Commission Awards Gatekeeper Automated Streetcar Enforcement System Contract
data.description:
AI-assisted video analytics and automated ticketing solution for the third largest public transit system in North America

Abbotsford, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - February 5, 2025) - Gatekeeper Systems Inc. (OTC Pink: GKPRF) ("Gatekeeper" or the "Company"), a leader in intelligent video and data solutions for public transportation and smart cities, is pleased to announce the Toronto Transit Commission (TTC) has awarded Gatekeeper its Automated Streetcar Enforcement System (ASES) pilot contract.

ASES is a video-based evidence capture and automated ticketing solution for enforcement of motorists that fail to stop for streetcars when the passenger doors are open. Gatekeeper will deploy its proprietary AI-assisted video analytics for incident detection, license plate capture, and automated ticket processing. The ASES pilot contract is for initial deployment on four streetcars and is valued at approximately $460,000.

The TTC operates streetcar routes in mixed-traffic conditions and certain stops require passengers to cross traffic lanes to board streetcars. Motorists are required by law to stop behind a streetcar once its doors are open, and regulations now allow the use of video to monitor and automatically enforce traffic violations against streetcars.

"Safety is our top concern, and every day, we hear about dozens of instances of cars passing open streetcar doors in mixed traffic with no regard for the safety of our customers," said TTC CEO Greg Percy. "We look forward to working with Gatekeeper Systems to develop a new, industry-leading technological solution to address that."

"We are delighted to work with the TTC on this important initiative to improve passenger safety," said Doug Dyment, Gatekeeper's President and CEO. "Protecting people in transit is our corporate mission and we are very proud to add Canada's largest public transit system to our growing list of transit customers."

The Toronto Transit Commission (TTC) is a city agency that provides public transit services to approximately 1.7 million daily commuters in Toronto and from surrounding municipalities. The TTC's mandate is to establish, operate and maintain the local passenger transportation system in the city of Toronto, which is the largest public transit system in Canada and the third largest in North America.

About Gatekeeper Systems Inc.

Gatekeeper is a leading provider of intelligent video and data solutions designed to provide a safer transportation environment for children, passengers, and public safety personnel on multiple transportation modes. The Company uses AI, video analytics, and Mobile Data Collectors to interconnect public transportation assets within a Smart City ecosystem. The Company's Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) business model is centered around wirelessly enabled Mobile Data Collectors which forms the foundation of the Company's data company evolution. www.gatekeeper-systems.com

Contact Information:

Douglas Dyment

President & CEO

ddyment@gatekeeper-systems.com

(604) 864-6187

data.title: Maersk to Initiate $2 Billion Share Buyback
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

A.P. Moeller-Maersk said it is initiating a share-buyback program of up to 14.4 billion danish Krona ($2 billion) over a period of 12 months.

The Danish shipping giant on Wednesday said that the first phase of the program will run from Feb. 7 up to Aug. 6, 2025, and will be limited to a total market value of 7.2 billion Danish Krona.

A maximum of 325,000 A shares and 1,900,000 B shares can be acquired in the first phase, it said.

The purpose of the program is to adjust the capital structure of the company, ultimately through the cancellation of repurchased shares, Maersk said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

Equifax Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
Equifax Board of Directors Declares Quarterly Dividend

PR Newswire

ATLANTA, Feb. 5, 2025

ATLANTA, Feb. 5, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Equifax(R) today announced that the Equifax Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.39 per share, payable on March 14, 2025, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on February 21, 2025. Equifax has paid cash dividends for more than 100 consecutive years.

ABOUT EQUIFAX INC.

At Equifax , we believe knowledge drives progress. As a global data, analytics, and technology company, we play an essential role in the global economy by helping financial institutions, companies, employers, and government agencies make critical decisions with greater confidence. Our unique blend of differentiated data, analytics, and cloud technology drives insights to power decisions to move people forward. Headquartered in Atlanta and supported by nearly 15,000 employees worldwide, Equifax operates or has investments in 24 countries in North America, Central and South America, Europe, and the Asia Pacific region. For more information, visit Equifax.com.

FOR MORE INFORMATION

MediaInquiries@equifax.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/equifax-board-of-directors-declares-quarterly-dividend-302369176.html

SOURCE Equifax Inc.

These share purchases form part of the on- and off-market limbs of the Company's existing share buy-back programme previously announced on 30 January 2025.

In respect of this programme, Natixis will make trading decisions in relation to the securities independently of the Company for a period from 30 January 2025 up to and including 25 April 2025.

The on-market limb will be effected within certain pre-set parameters and in accordance with the Company’s general authority to repurchase shares on-market. The off-market limb will be effected in accordance with the Company’s general authority to repurchase shares off-market pursuant to the off-market buyback contract approved by its shareholders and the pre-set parameters set out therein. The programme will be conducted in accordance with  Chapter 9 of the UK Listing Rules and Article 5 of the Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014/EU dealing with buy-back programmes (“EU MAR”) and EU MAR as “onshored” into UK law from the end of the Brexit transition period (at 11:00 pm on 31 December 2020) through the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (as amended by the European Union (Withdrawal Agreement) Act 2020), and as amended, supplemented, restated, novated, substituted or replaced by the Financial Services Act, 2021 and relevant statutory instruments (including, The Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations (SI 2019/310)), from time to time (“UK MAR”) and the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 (the “EU MAR Delegated Regulation”) and the EU MAR Delegated Regulation as “onshored” into UK law from the end of the Brexit transition period (at 11:00 pm on 31 December 2020) through the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (as amended by the European Union (Withdrawal Agreement) Act 2020), and as amended, supplemented, restated, novated, substituted or replaced by the Financial Services Act, 2021 and relevant statutory instruments (including, The Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations (SI 2019/310)), from time to time.

NASB Financial, Inc. Announces Financial Results
data.description:
NASB Financial, Inc. Announces Financial Results

PR Newswire

KANSAS CITY, Mo., Feb. 5, 2025

KANSAS CITY, Mo., Feb. 5, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — NASB Financial, Inc. (the "Company") announced today net income for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, of $8.0 million or $1.10 per share. This compares to net income of $6.1 million or $0.84 per share for the quarter ended September 30, 2024, and compares to net income of $7.6 million or $1.03 per share for the quarter ended December 31, 2023.

NASB Financial, Inc. is a unitary thrift holding company for North American Savings Bank, F.S.B. ("NASB"). Since 1927, NASB has been serving the financial needs of customers by providing an array of personal banking and lending products in the Kansas City metro area. NASB offers competitive residential and commercial mortgages nationwide with the safety and security of a Federal institution. For more information, visit nasb.com.

data.title: US Stocks Erase Losses
data.description:
US stock indices rebounded from early losses on Wednesday, supported by a sharp decline in long-dated Treasury yields as markets assessed a batch of mixed earnings releases and economic data.

The S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Dow Jones all inched around 0.3% higher.

Broadcom and Nvidia soared by 6% and 4.5%, respectively, to set a strong pace for chip makers.

Defensive stocks also performed well with gains by banks, healthcare, and consumer goods after a softer than expected ISM Services PMI consolidating bets of multiple Fed rate cuts this year.

On the other hand, the ADP report showed that more jobs were added to the private sector than expected, underscoring the robustness of the labor market.

In turn, Alphabet sank by 7.5% after the company missed cloud revenue expectations and announced higher-than-expected AI spending plans.

Also, AMD fell over 10% due to lower data center revenue and Uber fell 7% after weak first-quarter guidance.

data.title: Sofia share indices close mixed, SOFIX rises on on CCB, BSE gains
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), February 5 (SeeNews) - The blue-chip SOFIX index of the Bulgarian Stock Exchange (BSE) gained 0.39% to 892.82 points on Wednesday, while the remaining four indices closed evenly split, bourse data showed.

The benchmark index was supported by a 4.32% leap in Central Cooperative Bank [BUL:CCB] to 1.69 levs and a 2.03% climb in the operator of the Sofia bourse [BUL:BSE] to 7.55 levs.

The two companies also lifted the BGTR30, in which companies with a free float of at least 10% have equal weight. The index zoomed 0.27% to 861.07 points, additionally boosted by a 3.39% rise in organic foods producer and distributor Smart Organic [BUL:SO] to 24.40 levs.

The BEAMX index, which tracks the ten most highly capitalised growth companies, swelled 1.01% to 99.41 points on the back of an 8.00% surge in MFG Invest [BUL:MFG] to 2.70 levs.

Meanwhile, the BGBX40, which tracks the 40 most traded shares on the Sofia bourse, shrank 0.10% to 171.03 points due to a 10.37% plunge in real estate investment trust Sopharma Buildings REIT [BUL:SFB] to 12.10 levs.

The loss in Sopharma Buildings further drove down the BGREIT, which tracks seven real estate investment trusts, by 0.31% to 213.82 points.

Total turnover on the main market of the BSE dropped to 813,874 levs ($433,687/416,126 euro) on Wednesday from 3.89 million levs on Tuesday.

Battery manufacturer Monbat [BUL:MONB] generated the day's highest turnover, of 280,000 levs, after 100,000 shares traded. The stock closed 1.41% lower at 2.80 levs.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: Ireland's CRH to exit stake in SigmaRoc, says bookrunner
data.description:
Dublin-based building materials supplier CRH's UK unit intends to sell about 171.6 million ordinary shares in SigmaRoc , exiting its entire 15.4% stake in the British quarrying company, a bookrunner said on Wednesday.

In 2023, CRH sold its European lime operations, including sites in Ireland, the United Kingdom, Germany, the Czech Republic and Poland, to SigmaRoc for $1.1 billion.

Following CRH's stake exit, Fidelity Management & Research Company LLC with 58.3 million ordinary shares would become the largest shareholder in SigmaRoc, as per data from LSEG.

($1 = 0.9604 euros)

data.title: JPMorgan Chase to Expand in Alabama With 24 New Branches
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase said Wednesday it intends to expand operations in Alabama by opening 24 new branches, bringing the total to 35 by 2030.

An additional 170 bankers will be hired to support the expansion, according to the company.
